An internal service which serves no functionality to developers.

An object that emits sound. This object can be placed within a `Class.BasePart` or `Class.Attachment` to emit a sound from a particular position within a place or world, or it can be attached elsewhere to play the sound at a constant volume throughout the entire place.
SoundEffect is the base class that all other sound effects derive from. A SoundEffect can be applied to either a `Class.Sound` or `Class.SoundGroup` by being parented to either.

A `Class.SoundGroup` is used to manage the volume and sound effects on multiple `Class.Sound|Sounds` at once. `Class.Sound|Sounds` in the SoundGroup will have their volume and effects adjusted by the SoundGroup.
A service that determines various aspects of how the audio engine works. Most of its properties affect how `Class.Sound|Sounds` play in the experience.
A particle emitter with the visual aesthetic of sparkles.
An internal service that is responsible for the behavior of `Class.SpawnLocation|SpawnLocations`. Its functionality is not accessible to developers.

`Class.SpawnLocation|SpawnLocations`, or "spawns" determine where a `Class.Player` respawns when they die. They can be configured to allow only certain players to use each spawn, using `Class.Team|Teams`. They also control how `Class.ForceField|ForceFields` are set up for newly-spawned players.
The `Class.SpecialMesh` object applies a mesh to a `Class.BasePart` depending on the `Class.SpecialMesh.MeshType|MeshType` property.
A sphere-shaped handle that can be adorned to a `Class.BasePart`.
A light source that emits light directionally in the shape of a cone with a spherical base.
Simulates spring and damper behavior between two attachments.
Stores instances to be parented to a player's character when it spawns.

If the game allows gear, `StarterGear` is a container automatically inserted into each `Class.Player` object when the player joins the game. Whenever the player's character spawns, the contents of that player's `StarterGear` are copied into the player's `Class.Backpack`.
A container for `Class.LayerCollector` objects to be copied into the `Class.PlayerGui` of `Class.Player|Players`. Also provides a range of functions for interacting with the `Class.CoreGui`.
A container whose contents are copied into each player's `Class.Backpack` when their player character spawns. It is generally used to hold `Class.Tool|Tools`.
A service which allows the defaults of properties in the `Class.Player` object to be set.
A container for objects to be copied to a Player's PlayerScripts when they join a game.
Performance metrics for a game.
A single performance metric.
An unfinished object which offers no functionality to developers.
A container object for a single string.
Service allowing you to control Studio's Device Simulator.
Provides access to configuration of Roblox Studio and allows importing files from the user's file system.
Service allowing plugins to automate and customize Test and Run mode testing.
The base class for `Class.StyleSheet` and `Class.StyleRule`.
When parented to a `Class.StyleSheet`, references another `Class.StyleSheet` from which the parent inherits `Class.StyleRule|StyleRules` and token definitions.
Links a `Class.StyleSheet` to the instance tree whose root is the parent of the `StyleLink`.
Instance used to set conditions such as `"MaxSize"` and `"PreferredInput"` for a `Class.StyleRule`.
Defines style properties which override properties on the instances affected by the `Class.StyleRule.Selector|Selector` property.
Aggregates `Class.StyleRule|StyleRules` and can be linked to `Class.DataModel` trees to apply style properties to instances.
Renders dynamic rays from the sun.
An object that allows developers to override the appearance of a MeshPart with advanced graphics options.
Container for GuiObjects that are rendered on the surface of a part.
A light source that emits illumination of a specified color and brightness from a face for a specified range.
Highlights a face of a surface in a configurable color.
Collection of settings for the _Task Scheduler_ feature.
The `Class.Team` class represents a faction in a Roblox place. The only valid parent for a Team is in the `Class.Teams` service.
The Teams service holds a game's `Class.Team` objects. `Class.Team` objects must be parented to the Teams service.
The return structure of the `TeleportAsync` function call.
Optional input arguments to the `Class.TeleportService:TeleportAsync()` function.
Enables transporting `Class.Player|Players` between places and servers. For more information on how to teleport players between servers, see [Teleport between places](../../../projects/teleport.md).
`Terrain` lets you to create dynamically morphable environments.
Determines appearance of a certain terrain face direction.
A service used by Roblox to run controlled tests of the engine. It is available for developers to use, to a limited degree.
A 2D user interface element that displays player-editable text.
A 2D user interface element that displays interactive text.
Represents a text chat channel.
Represents a text chat command.
The parental class of all text chat configurations.
A data object representing a text chat message.
Overrides `Class.TextChatMessage` properties when returned by callbacks defined in `Class.TextChatService.OnIncomingMessage` or `Class.TextChannel.OnIncomingMessage`.
A service handling in-experience text chat.
Gives access to a large language model for text generation.
A 2D user interface element that displays non-interactive text.
Service internally responsible for handling the display of text.
Represents a speaker in a `Class.TextChannel`.
Applies a repeating image texture to the face of a parent `Class.BasePart`.
An internal service responsible for scheduling timed events. It is used by the `Class.Debris` class. Its functionality can not be accessed by developers.
An object, such as a weapon, that can be equipped by a `Class.Humanoid`.
Applies constant torque to an assembly from its center of mass.
A rotational spring that opposes the angular motion between two axes.
Measures a total-count-over-time interval.
An internal service responsible for touch inputs on mobile devices.
An internal object used by networking and replication code to transmit `Class.BasePart.Touched` and `Class.BasePart.TouchEnded` events.
Used to create a trail effect between two attachments.
The role of a Translator is to manufacture/return strings localized for the viewing player.
Creates a trembling effect on a sound by varying the volume of the sound up and down.
Abstract intermediate class that manages physical geometry properties for PartOperations and MeshParts.
Similar to a `Class.Part` but with a different visual `Class.TrussPart.Style|Style` and the important distinction that default characters are able to climb it.
The `Class.Tween` object controls the playback of an interpolation.
Abstract base class for in-between interpolation handlers. `Class.Tween` inherits from `Class.TweenBase`.
Used to create `Class.Tween|Tweens` which interpolate, or tween, the properties of instances.
Ensures the parent UI element maintains a particular aspect ratio.
`Class.UIBase` is the base class for UI layout and constraint classes.
A base class for UI constraint and layout classes.
The base class for UI constraint classes.
UI modifier which applies deformation to corners of its parent `Class.GuiObject`.
Instance which facilitates and encourages interaction with UI elements in an experience.
Defines flex behavior for a `Class.GuiObject` within a `Class.UIListLayout`.
Applies a color and transparency gradient to the UI elements rendered by the parent `Class.GuiObject`.
Positions sibling UI elements by filling rows using the space of the parent UI element.
The base class for grid style UI layouts.
The base class for UI layout classes.
Positions sibling UI elements in rows or columns within the parent UI container.
Applies padding to the borders of the parent `Class.GuiObject`.
An object that acts as a multiplier for the size of the parent UI element's scale.
Renders a shadow below the parent UI instance.
Ensures a `Class.GuiObject` does not become larger or smaller than the constraint's maximum size or minimum size.
Applies an outline to text or a UI border.
Lays out sibling UI elements and their child UI elements as rows/columns and cells in a table.

Ensures that the size of text rendered by certain `Class.GuiObject` classes lies within the range described by `Class.UITextSizeConstraint.MaxTextSize|MaxTextSize` and `Class.UITextSizeConstraint.MinTextSize|MinTextSize`.
Result of parts that have been joined together into a single solid model.
Ensures two axes on two bodies remain perpendicular.
An object which facilitates asynchronous, unordered and unreliable, one-way communication across the client-server boundary. Scripts firing a `Class.UnreliableRemoteEvent` do not yield.

The UserGameSettings is a singleton class found inside of the `Class.UserSettings` singleton. It holds various persistent settings relating to how the user wants to control their camera, and their character.
`UserInputService` is primarily used to detect the input types available on a user's device, as well as detect input events.
A service that handles queries regarding users on the Roblox platform.
A singleton object that houses basic user settings, which persist across all games on Roblox.
Base class of all "value instance" objects.
A sorted list of time-value pairs that define a curve. Used to animate a any type of value.
Represents a 3D vector curve, grouping three `Class.FloatCurve` instances.
A container object for a single Vector3 value.
Applies constant force to an assembly.
A seat object that can be used to control a vehicle.
A child class of `Class.Capture` for videos.
A GUI object that displays video content from a connected `Class.VideoPlayer`.
A GUI object that renders a rectangle, like a `Class.Frame` does, with a moving video image.
Used to play video assets.
An object for sampling frames from video content.
An internal service that offers no functionality to developers.
`Class.GuiObject` that renders 3D objects inside its bounds.
Simulates mouse, keyboard, and pointer input as if it were performed by a real player.
An internal service that offers no functionality to developers.
VirtualUser is a service that allows you to record the inputs of a user, and then play it back to a limited extent.
The Visit service is a backend service used by Roblox. Its functions are not accessible to developers in any form.
**VoiceChatService** is responsible for voice chat's high-level functionality.
Service responsible for handling interactions between Roblox and Virtual Reality (VR).
Maintains a streaming connection.
A type of `Class.BasePart` that has a wedge shape.
Connects two `Class.BasePart|BaseParts` together such that their relative position and orientation remain the same.
Connects one or more `Class.Instance|Instances` to form a processing graph of their streams. At the moment, only audio streams are supported, but this may expand in the future.
Renders a wireframe adornment consisting of one or more lines onto a `Class.BasePart` (including `Class.Terrain`) or into the `Class.Workspace`.
**Workspace** houses 3D objects which are rendered to the 3D world. Objects not descending from it will not be rendered or physically interact with the world.
Extends limited physics for its children on to a parent `Class.ViewportFrame`.
Base class for handling physics simulation and 3D spatial queries.
Allows for the real-time deformation of a `Class.MeshPart`.

The WrapLayer object defines a 3D accessory's inner and outer surfaces and other properties related to layering accessories. These surfaces, or the Inner Cage and Outer Cage, are similar to collision boxes, and describe the surfaces of which other 3D accessories can be placed without clipping or breaking.
The WrapTarget object defines a target. A target is the 3D body with only an outer surface, or an Outer Cage.
`Class.WrapTextureTransfer` allows a parent `Class.Decal` to be wrapped around its parent `Class.MeshPart` based on the cage of its `Class.WrapTarget`.
